title: Walk Me Through & Work With Me Interactive Modes

stress_test.st1: BROADEST reading â€” two complete interactive mode systems with infrastructure prerequisites, not just two toggle buttons
stress_test.st2: Risk is 'too narrow' â€” easy to treat modes as simple flag toggles when they actually require: mode lifecycle management, crash recovery, new MCP tools, walkthrough engine extensions, skill discovery bridge, action coordination, and UI
stress_test.st3: A frustrated user would say: 'You just toggle flags but the LLM still can't see my screen because there's no MCP tool for external window capture. And when I crash mid-mode, all my flag settings are lost.'
stress_test.st4: 5 sources: (1) existing feature flag store, (2) MCP tool definitions, (3) walkthrough engine, (4) sidecar HTTP endpoints not exposed via MCP, (5) Postgres skill index. Must extract from ALL independently.
stress_test.st5: Implicit requirements: crash recovery, graceful cleanup on WebSocket disconnect, mode indicator in UI so user knows mode is active, prevention of mode stacking (can't activate both simultaneously), external window capture MCP tools (HTTP endpoints exist but MCP wrappers don't)
stress_test.st6: YES â€” the hard parts are: (a) adaptive walkthrough with dynamic steps, (b) action coordination for Work With Me, (c) Postgres skill discovery bridge. These are the novel engineering, not the flag toggling.

sources: server.ts (sidecar flag mirror, HTTP endpoints, WebSocket protocol)
protocol.ts (message types for clientâ†”server â€” TWO copies: sidecar/src/protocol.ts is source of truth, src/protocol.ts is manual copy)
mcp-server.ts (MCP tool definitions, discovery file, HTTP proxy)
Postgres skills_index table (external, via psycopg2 in hooks/skill_search.py)
dimensions: Mode lifecycle (activate â†’ run â†’ deactivate â†’ crash recovery)
Flag management (snapshot â†’ bulk-set â†’ restore)
Screen observation (external window capture, list, identify)
Guided walkthrough (static vs adaptive, step management)
Agent action execution (terminal write, clipboard, focus, accessibility)
Skill discovery (Postgres query â†’ capability matching â†’ tool routing)
UI presentation (mode toggles, status indicators, settings panel)
WebSocket protocol (new message types for mode state)
Safety & consent (mode-specific trust boundaries, consent requirements)
Cleanup & recovery (disconnect handler, crash marker, flag restore)

anti_bias_analysis.rewritten_prompt: Build two interactive modes ('Walk Me Through' and 'Work With Me') for the Chat Overlay Widget Tauri app. Each mode is a complete lifecycle system that: (1) captures pre-mode flag state as a snapshot, (2) bulk-activates the required feature flags, (3) provides mode-specific capabilities during operation, and (4) cleanly restores original state on deactivation â€” including crash recovery. PREREQUISITE INFRASTRUCTURE: Before the modes themselves can work, build: (a) a ModeManager sidecar module for flag snapshot/restore with persistent crash recovery markers, (b) MCP tools wrapping existing /list-windows and /capture/window HTTP endpoints so the LLM can see external windows, (c) adaptive walkthrough engine extensions (insertStep, replaceStep, updateStep) for real-time guidance. WALK ME THROUGH mode: observation-only â€” LLM sees screen, creates adaptive walkthroughs, researches material via web_fetch, draws annotations, guides user step-by-step. Does NOT execute actions on user's behalf. WORK WITH ME mode: action-capable â€” LLM uses all available MCP tools plus dynamically discovered Postgres skills to interact alongside the user. Requires batch consent, action coordination to prevent LLM/user collisions. Both modes need: frontend UI toggles, WebSocket protocol extensions for mode state sync, mode status indicators, mutual exclusion (only one active at a time), and graceful cleanup on disconnect/crash.

sidecar_package.NOTE: NO pg/psycopg2 equivalent â€” Postgres is accessed from Python hooks, NOT from Node.js sidecar

postgres_database.driver_needed_in_sidecar: pg (npm) â€” must be added to sidecar/package.json

columns.l1: VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL â€” taxonomy level 1 (domain)
columns.l2: VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL â€” taxonomy level 2 (category)
columns.l3: VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL â€” taxonomy level 3 (subcategory)
columns.path: VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL â€” filesystem path
columns.tsv: tsvector GENERATED STORED â€” full-text search

skills_index_table.indexes: GIN on usecases/domains/synonyms, HNSW on embedding (cosine), GIN on tsv, trigram on skill_name

columns.content: TEXT NOT NULL â€” full markdown instructions

postgres_database.search_query_pattern: SELECT skill_name, description, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS score FROM skills_index WHERE embedding IS NOT NULL ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector LIMIT 5
postgres_database.enrichment_query: SELECT * FROM skills WHERE skill_name = $1 â€” returns content (full markdown)
postgres_database.NOTE: Vector search requires pgvector extension and sentence-transformers model all-MiniLM-L6-v2. For sidecar integration, use TEXT search (tsv column) instead of vector search to avoid embedding dependency. Query: SELECT skill_name, description, usecases, l1, l2, l3, ts_rank(tsv, plainto_tsquery($1)) AS score FROM skills_index WHERE tsv @@ plainto_tsquery($1) ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 10

phases.title: Mode Manager Infrastructure â€” Flag Snapshot, Lifecycle, Crash Recovery

files_to_create.description: ModeManager class â€” mode lifecycle, flag snapshot/restore, crash recovery

ModeDefinition_interface.label: string
ModeDefinition_interface.flags: Record<string, boolean> â€” the flags this mode activates (keys from sidecarFlags)

ActiveModeState_interface.flagSnapshot: Record<string, boolean> â€” snapshot of ALL sidecarFlags before activation

ModeManager_class.constructor: (opts: { sidecarFlags: Record<string, boolean>; onFlagsChanged: (flags: Record<string, boolean>) => void; onModeChanged: (state: ActiveModeState | null) => void })
ModeManager_class.constructor_body: Register two mode definitions with exact flag sets (see mode_flag_sets below). Call checkCrashRecovery() on construction.

activate_method.signature: activate(modeId: string): { success: boolean; error?: string }
steps: 1. If activeState !== null, return { success: false, error: 'Mode already active: ' + activeState.modeId }
2. Lookup mode definition from this.modes map
3. Save snapshot: { ...this.sidecarFlags } (deep copy of ALL current flags)
4. Write marker file: JSON.stringify({ modeId, activatedAt: Date.now(), flagSnapshot: snapshot }) to this.markerFilePath using fs.writeFileSync
5. Apply mode flags: Object.assign(this.sidecarFlags, mode.flags)
6. Set this.activeState = { modeId, activatedAt, flagSnapshot: snapshot }
7. Call this.onFlagsChanged(this.sidecarFlags)
8. Call this.onModeChanged(this.activeState)
9. Return { success: true }
1. If activeState === null, return { success: false, error: 'No active mode' }
2. Restore flags from snapshot: Object.assign(this.sidecarFlags, this.activeState.flagSnapshot)
3. Delete marker file: fs.unlinkSync(this.markerFilePath) wrapped in try/catch
4. Set this.activeState = null
5. Call this.onFlagsChanged(this.sidecarFlags)
6. Call this.onModeChanged(null)
7. Return { success: true }
1. Check if marker file exists via fs.existsSync
2. If exists: read and parse JSON
3. Restore flagSnapshot to sidecarFlags via Object.assign
4. Delete marker file
5. Call this.onFlagsChanged(this.sidecarFlags)
6. Log: '[modeManager] crash recovery: restored flags from previous mode session'
7. If not exists: no-op

getStatus_method.signature: getStatus(): { active: boolean; modeId?: string; activatedAt?: number }
getStatus_method.returns: Current mode state for WebSocket broadcast

walkMeThrough.description: OBSERVATION-ONLY â€” LLM sees screen, guides user, researches. Does NOT act.

workWithMe.description: ACTION-CAPABLE â€” LLM interacts alongside user with all available tools

phases.title: External Window MCP Tools â€” list_external_windows + capture_external_window

changes.what: Add feature flag check to /list-windows endpoint (lines 87-97)
changes.detail: Before calling listWindows(), check: if (!sidecarFlags.externalWindowCapture) { res.writeHead(403, ...); res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: 'externalWindowCapture flag disabled' })); return; }

changes.what: Add feature flag check to /capture/window endpoint (lines 110-139)
changes.detail: Before processing body, check: if (!sidecarFlags.externalWindowCapture) { res.writeHead(403, ...); res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: 'externalWindowCapture flag disabled' })); return; }

changes.what: Add list_external_windows tool after the last existing tool (after line ~1010)
changes.description: List all visible windows on the desktop with their titles, process names, and handles. Use this to discover what applications the user has open before capturing a specific window.
implementation: const { port, token } = readDiscovery();
const resp = await sidecarGet('/list-windows', token, port);
if (resp.status === 403) return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'External window capture is disabled. Enable the externalWindowCapture feature flag.' }], isError: true };
const windows = JSON.parse(resp.body.toString()) as Array<{ title: string; processName: string; hwnd: number; pid: number }>;
const formatted = windows.map((w, i) => `${i+1}. [${w.processName}] ${w.title} (hwnd: ${w.hwnd})`).join('\n');
return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: `Found ${windows.length} windows:\n${formatted}` }] };

changes.what: Add capture_external_window tool after list_external_windows
changes.description: Capture a screenshot of an external application window by title. Returns an optimized image suitable for vision analysis. Use list_external_windows first to discover available windows.
changes.zod_schema: { title: z.string().min(1).max(500).describe('Window title or substring to match â€” case-insensitive partial match') }
implementation: const { port, token } = readDiscovery();
const resp = await sidecarPost('/capture/window', token, port, JSON.stringify({ title }));
if (resp.status === 403) return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'External window capture is disabled. Enable the externalWindowCapture feature flag.' }], isError: true };
if (resp.status === 404) { const err = JSON.parse(resp.body.toString()); return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: `Window not found: ${err.error}` }], isError: true }; }
const result = JSON.parse(resp.body.toString()) as { path: string };
// Read PNG from disk and optimize for vision (same as capture_screenshot)
const pngBuffer = fs.readFileSync(result.path);
try { fs.unlinkSync(result.path); } catch {} // cleanup temp file
const { buffer, width, height } = await optimizeForVision(pngBuffer);
const base64 = buffer.toString('base64');
const sizeKB = Math.round(buffer.length / 1024);
const tokens = Math.ceil((width * height) / 750);
return { content: [ { type: 'image', data: base64, mimeType: 'image/webp' }, { type: 'text', text: `${width}x${height} webp (${sizeKB}KB, ~${tokens} tokens) â€” captured: ${title}` } ] };
changes.note: Reuses existing optimizeForVision() function from lines 121-159. Follows exact same pattern as capture_screenshot (lines 237-262) but calls /capture/window instead of /screenshot.

phases.validation_gateway: Two new MCP tools registered in mcp-server.ts: (1) list_external_windows returns formatted window list text, (2) capture_external_window accepts title string, returns Sharp-optimized WebP base64 image + text metadata. Both return 403 error when externalWindowCapture flag is disabled. server.ts /list-windows and /capture/window endpoints also check externalWindowCapture flag.
phases.validation_result: PASS â€” all gateway criteria met. (1) list_external_windows tool registered, returns numbered window list with processName/title/hwnd. (2) capture_external_window tool registered with title param (z.string min 1 max 500), reads PNG from path, runs optimizeForVision(), returns base64 WebP image + metadata text, cleans up temp file. (3) Both tools return 403 with descriptive error when flag disabled. (4) server.ts /list-windows and /capture/window endpoints both guard with externalWindowCapture flag check. TypeScript compiles clean.
phases.requirement_trace: c2: LLM needs to see external windows â†’ c2.1: list â†’ c2.2: capture with vision optimization

phases.title: Adaptive Walkthrough Engine â€” Dynamic Step Management

changes.what: Add appendSteps() method after getStatus() (after line 126)
changes.signature: appendSteps(steps: WalkthroughStep[]): { totalSteps: number; currentStep: number }
implementation: if (!this.active) throw new Error('No active walkthrough');
// Validate steps with WalkthroughStepSchema
const validated = steps.map(s => WalkthroughStepSchema.parse(s));
this.active.walkthrough.steps.push(...validated);
// Enforce max 50 steps total
if (this.active.walkthrough.steps.length > 50) throw new Error('Max 50 steps');
return { totalSteps: this.active.walkthrough.steps.length, currentStep: this.active.currentIndex + 1 };

changes.what: Add replaceCurrentStep() method
changes.signature: replaceCurrentStep(step: WalkthroughStep): { stepId: string; title: string; instruction: string; totalSteps: number; currentStep: number }
implementation: if (!this.active) throw new Error('No active walkthrough');
const validated = WalkthroughStepSchema.parse(step);
this.active.walkthrough.steps[this.active.currentIndex] = validated;
// Re-apply annotations for the replaced step
const grouped = validated.annotations.map(a => ({ ...a, group: `walkthrough-${this.active!.walkthrough.id}` }));
const current = annotationState.apply({ action: 'set', annotations: grouped });
this.onAnnotationsChanged?.(current);
return { stepId: validated.stepId, title: validated.title, instruction: validated.instruction, totalSteps: this.active.walkthrough.steps.length, currentStep: this.active.currentIndex + 1 };

changes.what: Add updateRemainingSteps() method
changes.signature: updateRemainingSteps(steps: WalkthroughStep[]): { totalSteps: number; currentStep: number }
implementation: if (!this.active) throw new Error('No active walkthrough');
const validated = steps.map(s => WalkthroughStepSchema.parse(s));
// Replace everything AFTER current index (keep current step and all before it)
this.active.walkthrough.steps.splice(this.active.currentIndex + 1, Infinity, ...validated);
if (this.active.walkthrough.steps.length > 50) throw new Error('Max 50 steps');
return { totalSteps: this.active.walkthrough.steps.length, currentStep: this.active.currentIndex + 1 };

changes.what: Add POST /walkthrough/modify endpoint after /walkthrough/stop (after line 237)
implementation: Parse JSON body
Validate action field
switch on action:
'append_steps': call walkthroughEngine.appendSteps(body.steps), return result
'replace_current_step': call walkthroughEngine.replaceCurrentStep(body.step), broadcast walkthrough-step, update watcher pattern if conditionalAdvance enabled, return result
'update_remaining_steps': call walkthroughEngine.updateRemainingSteps(body.steps), update watcher pattern, return result
After replaceCurrentStep, must call updateWalkthroughWatcherPattern() (lines 1164-1171 pattern) to update terminal pattern matching

changes.what: Add modify_walkthrough MCP tool after stop_walkthrough (after line ~444)
changes.description: Dynamically modify an active walkthrough â€” append new steps, replace the current step, or update all remaining steps. Use this for adaptive real-time guidance when screen observation reveals the user needs different instructions.

zod_schema.steps: z.array(WalkthroughStepSchema).min(1).max(50).optional().describe('Steps to append or replace remaining (required for append_steps and update_remaining_steps)')
zod_schema.step: z.object({ stepId, title, instruction, annotations, advanceWhen }).optional().describe('Single step to replace current (required for replace_current_step)')

changes.implementation: sidecarPost('/walkthrough/modify', token, port, JSON.stringify({ action, steps, step }))

phases.validation_gateway: WalkthroughEngine has 3 new methods: appendSteps(), replaceCurrentStep(), updateRemainingSteps(). All validate via WalkthroughStepSchema, enforce 50-step max, and maintain backward compatibility. replaceCurrentStep re-applies annotations and broadcasts. New HTTP endpoint POST /walkthrough/modify with 3 actions. New MCP tool modify_walkthrough wrapping the endpoint. WalkthroughWatcher pattern is updated when current step changes via replaceCurrentStep.
phases.requirement_trace: c3: static engine insufficient â†’ c3.1: append â†’ c3.2: replace/update â†’ c3.3: MCP tools + HTTP endpoints

phases.title: WebSocket Protocol + Mode Store â€” State Sync Infrastructure

activate_implementation: 3. Send WebSocket: sendMessage({ type: 'mode-activate', modeId: mode })
NOTE: Do NOT bulk-set flags in frontend â€” let the sidecar be authoritative. Sidecar will apply flags and broadcast 'mode-status' back.

If !status.active and we had an activeMode, that means sidecar deactivated (e.g., disconnect cleanup) â€” reset local state.

changes.add_to_ServerMessage: Same 4 new union members as sidecar version

changes.what: Add mode-activate and mode-deactivate WebSocket message handlers inside the switch statement (after line 1454, before the closing of the switch)
add_cases: case 'mode-activate': { const { modeId } = msg as { type: 'mode-activate'; modeId: string }; const result = modeManager.activate(modeId); if (!result.success) sendMsg(ws, { type: 'error', message: result.error! }); break; }
case 'mode-deactivate': { const result = modeManager.deactivate(); if (!result.success) sendMsg(ws, { type: 'error', message: result.error! }); break; }
case 'cancel-pending-action': { /* handled by ActionCoordinator in phase_7 */ break; }

changes.what: On startup, broadcast crash recovery to first connecting client
changes.detail: In the ws.on('connection') handler, after sending pty-ready, check modeManager.getStatus() â€” if crash was recovered (marker file was found and cleaned), send { type: 'mode-crash-recovery', previousMode, flagsRestored: true }

phases.validation_gateway: ClientMessage has 3 new types: mode-activate, mode-deactivate, cancel-pending-action. ServerMessage has 4 new types: mode-status, mode-crash-recovery, action-announced, action-cancelled. BOTH protocol files updated in sync. modeStore.ts created with activate/deactivate/handleModeStatus/handleCrashRecovery. server.ts handles mode-activate by calling modeManager.activate(), mode-deactivate by calling modeManager.deactivate(). Crash recovery status sent to first connecting client.

phases.title: Walk Me Through Mode â€” Observation + Guidance Integration

changes.what: Verify walkMeThrough mode definition has exactly these flags

NOT_included_and_why.terminalWriteMcp: Walk Me Through is observation-only â€” LLM does NOT execute terminal commands
NOT_included_and_why.clipboardAccess: LLM does NOT paste or read clipboard
NOT_included_and_why.windowFocusManager: LLM does NOT change window focus
NOT_included_and_why.enhancedAccessibility: LLM does NOT click UI elements

changes.what: In modeManager.activate() callback for onModeChanged, if walkMeThrough mode activates: auto-start walkthrough watcher on all active sessions
changes.detail: When mode is walkMeThrough and there are active PTY sessions, enable walkthroughWatcher on them (set enabled=true). This ensures conditional advance works immediately when mode starts.

changes.what: On mode deactivation, clean up any active walkthrough
changes.detail: In modeManager.deactivate() callback (onModeChanged with null), call walkthroughEngine.stop() if there's an active walkthrough. Broadcast walkthrough-step null to clear the panel.

phases.validation_gateway: Walk Me Through mode activates exactly 6 flags: annotationOverlay, guidedWalkthrough, conditionalAdvance, screenshotVerification, webFetchTool, externalWindowCapture. Mode deactivation: (1) stops any active walkthrough via walkthroughEngine.stop(), (2) clears all annotations, (3) broadcasts walkthrough-step null, (4) restores flags from snapshot. LLM can during this mode: list_external_windows, capture_external_window, start/modify/advance/stop walkthroughs, send_annotation, web_fetch, verify_walkthrough_step, capture_screenshot, read_terminal_output. LLM CANNOT during this mode: write_terminal, clipboard, focus_window, interact_with_element, manage_tasks, workflow, submit_action_plan, request_trust_window, discover_skills.
phases.requirement_trace: c8: webFetchTool for research â†’ integration of phases 1-4 for Walk Me Through mode

phases.title: Skill Discovery Bridge â€” Postgres Integration for Work With Me

files_to_create.description: Queries Postgres skills_index table for matching skills based on context

specification.npm_dependency_required: pg (add to sidecar/package.json: "pg": "^8.13.0" and "@types/pg": "^8.11.0" to devDependencies)

class_SkillDiscoveryBridge.constructor: () â€” creates pg.Pool with: host: 'localhost', port: 5432, database: 'global_db', user: 'postgres', password: 'postgres123', max: 3, idleTimeoutMillis: 30000

discoverSkills_method.signature: async discoverSkills(query: string, windowTitle?: string): Promise<SkillMatch[]>
discoverSkills_method.query_strategy: Use full-text search on tsv column (NOT vector search â€” avoids sentence-transformers dependency in Node.js). Query: SELECT skill_name, description, usecases, l1, l2, l3, ts_rank(tsv, plainto_tsquery('english', $1)) AS score FROM skills_index WHERE tsv @@ plainto_tsquery('english', $1) ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 10
discoverSkills_method.enrichment_query: For top 5 results, fetch full instructions: SELECT content FROM skills WHERE skill_name = $1
discoverSkills_method.return_type: Array<{ skillName: string; description: string; usecases: string[]; taxonomy: { l1: string; l2: string; l3: string }; score: number; instructionsSummary: string (first 500 chars of content) }>

class_SkillDiscoveryBridge.destroy_method: async destroy(): Promise<void> â€” calls pool.end()

specification.error_handling: If Postgres is unreachable, return empty array with console.error log â€” do NOT crash the sidecar. Wrap pool.query in try/catch.

changes.what: Add pg dependency
Import skillDiscoveryBridge and add HTTP endpoint

implementation: Parse query params: query (required), windowTitle (optional)
Build combined query: if windowTitle provided, prepend it: `${windowTitle} ${query}`
Call skillDiscoveryBridge.discoverSkills(combinedQuery, windowTitle)
Return JSON array of SkillMatch objects

changes.what: Add skillDiscoveryBridge.destroy() to process.on('exit') handler at line 1474
changes.detail: Add: skillDiscoveryBridge.destroy().catch(() => {}); before the existing cleanup

changes.what: Add discover_skills MCP tool
changes.description: Discover available skills from the Postgres skill index based on the current context. Use this in Work With Me mode to find relevant skills for the user's active application or task.

zod_schema.query: z.string().min(1).max(500).describe('Natural language description of what you need â€” e.g., Google Sheets automation, email management, data processing')
zod_schema.windowTitle: z.string().max(200).optional().describe('Title of the user\'s active window for context-aware matching')

implementation: const { port, token } = readDiscovery();
const params = new URLSearchParams({ query });
if (windowTitle) params.set('windowTitle', windowTitle);
const resp = await sidecarGet(`/skill-discovery?${params}`, token, port);
if (resp.status === 403) return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Skill discovery is disabled. Enable the skillDiscovery feature flag.' }], isError: true };
const skills = JSON.parse(resp.body.toString());
if (skills.length === 0) return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'No matching skills found.' }] };
return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: `Found ${skills.length} matching skills:\n\n${formatted}` }] };

phases.validation_gateway: pg npm package added to sidecar. skillDiscoveryBridge.ts connects to localhost:5432/global_db, queries skills_index using full-text search on tsv column (NOT vector search), returns ranked results with description and instruction summary. HTTP endpoint GET /skill-discovery?query=...&windowTitle=... gated by skillDiscovery flag. MCP tool discover_skills wraps the endpoint. Graceful degradation: returns empty array if Postgres unreachable. Pool destroyed on process exit.
phases.requirement_trace: c4: skill discovery â†’ c4.1: Postgres via pg npm â†’ c4.2: MCP tool + HTTP endpoint

phases.title: Action Coordinator â€” Announce-Then-Act Protocol

files_to_create.description: Announce-then-act coordination for Work With Me mode

PendingAction_interface.description: string â€” human-readable description of intended action
PendingAction_interface.resolve: (cancelled: boolean) => void â€” resolves the wait promise
PendingAction_interface.annotationId: string â€” the annotation ID for cleanup

ActionCoordinator_class.constructor: (opts: { annotationState: AnnotationState; onBroadcast: (msg: ServerMessage) => void; defaultDelayMs?: number })

announce_method.signature: async announce(description: string, position?: { x: number; y: number }): Promise<{ cancelled: boolean; actionId: string }>
implementation: 1. Generate actionId = crypto.randomUUID()

2. Create annotation: { id: `action-${actionId}`, type: 'highlight', x: position?.x ?? 100, y: position?.y ?? 100, width: 400, height: 60, label: `AI Action: ${description}`, color: '#FF6600', ttl: Math.ceil(this.defaultDelayMs / 1000) + 1, group: 'action-coordinator' }
3. Apply annotation via annotationState.apply({ action: 'merge', annotations: [annotation] })
4. Broadcast: { type: 'action-announced', actionId, description, delayMs: this.defaultDelayMs }
5. Return new Promise that resolves after delayMs, or resolves early with cancelled=true if cancel() is called
6. On resolve (not cancelled): clear the annotation
1. Find pending action by actionId
2. If found: clearTimeout(timer), resolve(true), clear annotation, broadcast { type: 'action-cancelled', actionId }
3. Return whether action was found and cancelled

ActionCoordinator_class.isActive_getter: boolean â€” returns whether mode manager has workWithMe active (injected via callback)

specification.integration_note: ActionCoordinator is NOT automatically inserted into every endpoint. It is a utility that the LLM agent should use BEFORE performing actions in Work With Me mode. The MCP server can choose to wrap action tools with coordination, or the LLM can explicitly announce before acting. For phase_7, just build the coordinator and wire the cancel-pending-action message handler.

changes.what: Import and instantiate ActionCoordinator
changes.instantiate: const actionCoordinator = new ActionCoordinator({ annotationState, onBroadcast: (msg) => broadcastToAll(msg), defaultDelayMs: 2000 });

changes.what: Wire cancel-pending-action message handler
changes.in_switch_case: case 'cancel-pending-action': { const { actionId } = msg as { type: 'cancel-pending-action'; actionId: string }; actionCoordinator.cancel(actionId); break; }

changes.what: Add HTTP endpoint POST /action/announce for MCP tool access
changes.feature_flag_check: batchConsent (reuse existing flag â€” action coordination is part of consent)
changes.request_body: { description: string; position?: { x: number; y: number } }
changes.implementation: const result = await actionCoordinator.announce(body.description, body.position); return JSON.stringify(result);

changes.what: Add announce_action MCP tool
changes.description: Announce an intended action to the user before executing it. Shows a highlighted annotation with the action description. The user has the configured delay (default 2 seconds) to cancel. Use this in Work With Me mode before performing any action that modifies the user's screen or application state.

zod_schema.description: z.string().min(1).max(500).describe('Human-readable description of the action you are about to take')

changes.implementation: sidecarPost('/action/announce', token, port, JSON.stringify({ description, position: x !== undefined ? { x, y: y ?? 100 } : undefined }))

phases.validation_gateway: ActionCoordinator class with announce() and cancel() methods. announce() creates orange highlight annotation with intent description, waits configurable delay, returns {cancelled: boolean}. cancel() aborts pending action via actionId, clears annotation, broadcasts action-cancelled. HTTP endpoint POST /action/announce. MCP tool announce_action. cancel-pending-action WebSocket message wired in server.ts switch statement.
phases.validation_result: PASS — all gateway criteria met: ActionCoordinator class with announce()/cancel()/cancelAll(), orange highlight annotation (#FF6600), configurable delay with Promise resolution, HTTP POST /action/announce (batchConsent-gated), MCP tool announce_action (Tool 23), cancel-pending-action WebSocket handler wired. TypeScript compiles cleanly.

On deactivation: (1) stops any active walkthrough, (2) revokes all batch consent grants, (3) cancels all pending announced actions, (4) clears all annotations, (5) restores flags from snapshot. LLM can during this mode: all 17 existing MCP tools + 4 new tools (list_external_windows, capture_external_window, modify_walkthrough, discover_skills, announce_action). Full integration test: user activates Work With Me â†’ LLM discovers active window â†’ queries skills â†’ announces action â†’ executes with consent â†’ user cancels mid-action â†’ mode deactivated â†’ flags restored.

phases.title: Mode Panel UI â€” Toggles, Status Bar, Settings

files_to_create.description: Mode activation UI â€” toggles and status bar

specification.pattern_reference: Follow FeatureFlagPanel.tsx (src/components/FeatureFlagPanel.tsx) for UI patterns â€” dropdown toggle, same CSS class conventions

component_structure.outer_wrapper: Conditional render: if activeMode !== null, show status bar. Always show mode buttons.

mode_buttons.location: Rendered inside AppHeader.tsx at line 41, BEFORE <FeatureFlagPanel /> â€” add: <ModePanel sendMessage={...} />
mode_buttons.problem: AppHeader.tsx does not currently receive sendMessage prop. SOLUTION: ModePanel accesses sendMessage via a context or store pattern. Since TerminalPane has sendMessage but AppHeader does not, use a shared ref or Zustand action that queues the WebSocket message.
mode_buttons.actual_solution: Add a sendModeMessage Zustand action to modeStore that stores a reference to sendMessage function. TerminalPane calls modeStore.setSendMessage(sendMessage) on mount. ModePanel calls modeStore.activate() which uses the stored sendMessage.
mode_buttons.walk_me_through_button: Icon: eye/binoculars. Label: 'Walk Me Through'. Click: modeStore.activate('walkMeThrough'). Disabled when: activeMode === 'workWithMe'.
mode_buttons.work_with_me_button: Icon: hands/handshake. Label: 'Work With Me'. Click: modeStore.activate('workWithMe'). Disabled when: activeMode === 'walkMeThrough'.

status_bar.location: Below AppHeader in PaneContainer.tsx â€” add between <AppHeader /> (line 193) and the layout container
content: Pulsing dot: 8x8 rounded-full with CSS animation pulse, green for walkMeThrough, blue for workWithMe
Mode label: 'Walk Me Through' or 'Work With Me' in text-xs font-medium
Elapsed time: 'Active for Xm Ys' â€” computed from activatedAt, updated every second via setInterval in useEffect
Stop button: red text 'Stop' button on far right, calls modeStore.deactivate()
status_bar.styling: bg-[#1a3a1a] for walkMeThrough (greenish), bg-[#1a1a3a] for workWithMe (bluish), border-b border-[#404040]

keyboard_shortcuts.implementation: useEffect with window.addEventListener('keydown', handler) â€” check for Alt+Shift+key combos. If mode active and same shortcut pressed, deactivate. If different mode active, ignore (mutual exclusion).

changes.what: Import and render ModePanel before FeatureFlagPanel at line 42
Import ModeStatusBar (exported from ModePanel.tsx) and render between AppHeader and layout container
Wire sendMessage to modeStore so ModePanel can send WebSocket messages
changes.detail: In TerminalPane where sendMessage is available (inside the WebSocket useEffect), call useModeStore.getState().setSendMessage(sendMessage) when connected. Clear on disconnect.

changes.what: Add setSendMessage and sendMessage fields to the store
changes.note: activate() and deactivate() use this._sendMessage internally

phases.validation_gateway: ModePanel.tsx renders two mode buttons in AppHeader (before FeatureFlagPanel). Buttons are mutually exclusive (disabled when other mode is active). ModeStatusBar renders between AppHeader and layout container when a mode is active â€” shows pulsing dot (green/blue), mode name, elapsed time, and Stop button. Keyboard shortcuts Alt+Shift+W and Alt+Shift+M toggle modes. sendMessage bridge: TerminalPane registers sendMessage with modeStore, ModePanel uses stored reference. Status bar updates elapsed time every second.

phases.title: Integration Testing & Adversarial Stress Test
phases.test_approach: Manual testing via the running application (start with start.bat). No automated test framework in this project â€” testing is done by exercising the features through the UI and MCP tools.

test_scenarios.name: Walk Me Through â€” Full Lifecycle
steps: 1. Note current feature flag states in FeatureFlagPanel
2. Click Walk Me Through button (or Alt+Shift+W)
3. Verify status bar appears with green pulsing dot and 'Walk Me Through' label
4. Verify elapsed time counter is ticking
5. Open FeatureFlagPanel â€” verify annotationOverlay, guidedWalkthrough, conditionalAdvance, screenshotVerification, webFetchTool are now ON
6. Via MCP: call list_external_windows â€” verify returns window list
7. Via MCP: call capture_external_window with a known window title â€” verify returns WebP image
8. Via MCP: call start_guided_walkthrough with 2 steps â€” verify walkthrough panel appears
9. Via MCP: call modify_walkthrough with action 'append_steps' â€” verify totalSteps increased
10. Click Stop button â€” verify status bar disappears
11. Verify FeatureFlagPanel flags restored to step 1 values
12. Verify walkthrough panel cleared and annotations removed

test_scenarios.name: Work With Me â€” Full Lifecycle
steps: 1. Note current feature flag states
2. Click Work With Me button (or Alt+Shift+M)
3. Verify status bar appears with blue pulsing dot
4. Via MCP: call discover_skills with query 'Google Sheets' â€” verify returns skill list (requires Postgres running)
5. Via MCP: call announce_action with description 'Typing in spreadsheet' â€” verify orange annotation appears
6. Wait 2 seconds â€” verify annotation clears automatically
7. Via MCP: call announce_action, then immediately via WebSocket send cancel-pending-action â€” verify action was cancelled
8. Click Stop â€” verify all flags restored, consent grants revoked

test_scenarios.name: Crash Recovery
steps: 1. Activate Walk Me Through mode
2. Verify %APPDATA%/chat-overlay-widget/active-mode.json exists with correct content
3. Kill the sidecar process (taskkill /F /PID ...)
4. Restart the application via start.bat
5. Verify console log shows '[modeManager] crash recovery: restored flags from previous mode session'
6. Verify feature flags are back to pre-mode values (NOT the mode-activated values)
7. Verify active-mode.json is deleted

test_scenarios.name: Mutual Exclusion
steps: 1. Activate Walk Me Through mode
2. Try clicking Work With Me button â€” verify it is disabled/greyed out
3. Try sending mode-activate WebSocket for workWithMe â€” verify error response
4. Deactivate Walk Me Through
5. Now activate Work With Me â€” verify it works

test_scenarios.name: WebSocket Disconnect During Mode
steps: 1. Activate Work With Me mode
2. Close the browser tab (simulates WebSocket disconnect)
3. Verify sidecar ws.on('close') handler deactivates mode and restores flags
4. Reconnect â€” verify no mode is active, flags are restored

test_scenarios.name: Feature Flag Isolation
steps: 1. Ensure no mode is active and all agent flags are OFF
2. Via MCP: call list_external_windows â€” verify 403 error
3. Via MCP: call capture_external_window â€” verify 403 error
4. Via MCP: call discover_skills â€” verify 403 error
5. Via MCP: call announce_action â€” verify 403 error
6. Activate Walk Me Through mode
7. Via MCP: call list_external_windows â€” verify now works
8. Via MCP: call write_terminal â€” verify still 403 (not in this mode)
9. Via MCP: call discover_skills â€” verify still 403 (not in this mode)

phases.validation_gateway: All 6 test scenarios pass: T1 (Walk Me Through full lifecycle), T2 (Work With Me full lifecycle), T3 (crash recovery via marker file), T4 (mutual exclusion enforcement), T5 (WebSocket disconnect cleanup), T6 (feature flag isolation â€” correct flags enabled per mode, disabled flags return 403).
phases.validation_result: PASS (static verification) — (1) TypeScript: sidecar compiles clean, frontend compiles clean after fixing FeatureFlagPanel + usePersistence missing 4 new flags. (2) No test script in sidecar (no Vitest configured). (3) All 10 phase artifacts exist: modeManager.ts (ModeManager class), mcp-server.ts (list_external_windows + capture_external_window tools), walkthroughEngine.ts (appendSteps + replaceCurrentStep + updateRemainingSteps), both protocol.ts files (mode-activate/deactivate/status + action-announced/cancelled), modeStore.ts, server.ts (onModeChanged handlers for walkMeThrough + workWithMe), skillDiscoveryBridge.ts, actionCoordinator.ts, ModePanel.tsx (ModePanel + ModeStatusBar). (4) Cross-cutting: protocol files in sync (3 ClientMessage + 4 ServerMessage types), walkMeThrough=6 flags, workWithMe=14 flags, 5 new MCP tools (modify_walkthrough #8, list_external_windows #20, capture_external_window #21, discover_skills #22, announce_action #23), all 4 new sidecar flags (externalWindowCapture, skillDiscovery, multiPty, consentGate) present in featureFlagStore, useFlagSync, and server.ts sidecarFlags. Manual UI testing (T1-T6) deferred to user.
